name: Rhubarb Meringue Torte
recipeInstructions:
One day before serving:
1. In a medium-sized bowl, beat egg whites and cream of tartar with an electric mixer on high speed until soft peaks form.
2. Gradually add sugar while continuing to beat until the mixture forms stiff peaks.
3. Preheat the oven to 225°F and line two baking sheets with greased aluminum foil.
4. Draw three circles, each measuring 8 inches in diameter, onto the baking sheets using a pastry bag fitted with a star tip.
5. Pipe a border around each circle, but do not extend beyond the edge of the circle.
6. In the center of one circle, pipe a second border inside the first one.
7. Divide the remaining meringue among the centers of the circles and spread it to touch the shell borders using a small spatula.
8. Bake the meringues for 3-4 hours or until crisp. Turn off the oven.
9. Allow the meringues to stand in the oven for several hours or overnight.
10. About an hour before serving, place a single-border meringue layer on a serving plate. Top with one-third of chilled rhubarb sauce.
11. Repeat with a second single-border meringue and one-third of the sauce.
12. Place the double-border meringue on top and add the remaining sauce.
13. Cover the torte carefully and refrigerate until ready to serve.
